response:
To compute the **sample standard deviation** of the data set:

$$
\{-11, 9, 5, 15, -10, -15, 12, -25, -22\}
$$

---

### Step 1: Find the mean (average) of the data set

$$
\text{Mean} = \frac{-11 + 9 + 5 + 15 - 10 - 15 + 12 - 25 - 22}{9}
$$

$$
\text{Mean} = \frac{-11 + 9 = -2, -2 + 5 = 3, 3 + 15 = 18, 18 - 10 = 8, 8 - 15 = -7, -7 + 12 = 5, 5 - 25 = -20, -20 - 22 = -42}{9}
$$

$$
\text{Mean} = \frac{-42}{9} = -\frac{14}{3} \approx -4.67
$$

---

### Step 2: Compute the squared differences from the mean

We calculate the squared difference for each value:

$$
\begin{align*}
(-11 - (-\frac{14}{3}))^2 &= \left(-\frac{35}{3}\right)^2 = \frac{1225}{9} \\
9 - (-\frac{14}{3}) &= \frac{43}{3} \Rightarrow \left(\frac{43}{3}\right)^2 = \frac{1849}{9} \\
5 - (-\frac{14}{3}) &= \frac{23}{3} \Rightarrow \left(\frac{23}{3}\right)^2 = \frac{529}{9} \\
15 - (-\frac{14}{3}) &= \frac{59}{3} \Rightarrow \left(\frac{59}{3}\right)^2 = \frac{3481}{9} \\
-10 - (-\frac{14}{3}) &= -\frac{16}{3} \Rightarrow \left(-\frac{16}{3}\right)^2 = \frac{256
